reason: Response A has the comprehensive, evenly-weighted, paragraph-based structure typical of genuine Haiku, covering each factor (lighting, color, sound, temperature, texture, spatial arrangement) in balanced prose without affecting a distinctive authorial voice. Response B leans hard into a punchy, aphoristic "voice" with short declarative fragments, contrarian framing ("less mysterious than people think," "the main enemy," "get out of the way") and rhetorical flourishes that read like a model deliberately performing a stylized persona rather than Haiku's more straightforward, helpful exposition. The slightly generic thoroughness of A is more characteristic of authentic Haiku output.
